program: Travel English -- Europe Without a Translator
hub_subtitle: Travel English for the airport, the check-in counter, the hotel and passport control — so that Europe needs no translator

lesson.menu_title: Something Went Wrong -- One Night in Madrid
lesson.menu_desc: Should, could and might when the room is gone, the bill is wrong and the flight is late -- how to complain and be helped, without shouting and without apologizing -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 9 -- Something Went Wrong
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rosângela | Lesson 9 -- Something Went Wrong
lesson.grammar_point: should, could and might for solving problems

listenings.text: Good evening, ladies and gentlemen. This is an announcement for the passengers on the eight o'clock flight to Lisbon. I am afraid this flight is delayed. The aircraft is still in Paris, and the new departure time might be ten fifteen tonight. I say might, because we do not know yet. We are going to give you more information at nine o'clock, and we are very sorry for the delay. Now, please listen, because the next part is important, and it is different for different people. First: you should stay near this gate. Do not go far. If the aircraft arrives early, we are going to board early, and we cannot wait for you. Second: if you have another flight after Lisbon tonight, do not wait here. You should go to desk number four now, and you should speak to an agent there. They could put you on a different flight this evening, or on the first flight tomorrow morning, and they could book a hotel room for you tonight. But you should go now, because at eleven o'clock that desk is closed. Third: a delay of more than three hours is not the same as a small delay. If we leave after eleven, you could have the right to compensation from the airline. You should keep your boarding pass, and you should keep it even after the flight, because without it you cannot ask for anything. Fourth: in ten minutes, a member of our staff is going to walk along this gate with water and sandwiches, and they are free. Please take one. On behalf of the airline, I would like to give you our apology for this evening. Thank you very much for your patience.

Hello. My name is Pilar, and I am the duty manager of this hotel, here in Madrid. Twenty-two years. I am the person you ask for when something goes wrong, so let me tell you a secret, and it is going to save you a lot of nights. The guests who shout get less. Not more. Less. I know, everybody thinks the opposite. But listen. When a man shouts at my reception desk at midnight, the first thing I feel is my heart going fast, and the second thing I do is follow the rules, exactly, word by word, because that is how I protect myself. And the rules give him nothing. Nothing at all. Now, the calm woman next to him. She says: there seems to be a problem with my reservation, I booked a double room in March, and I was told that it was confirmed. Could you check it again, please? What happens then? My hands are not shaking, my head is working, and my head knows things the rules do not know. I know we have a room on the fourth floor that we keep for the airline. I know the hotel across the street has space tonight, and I know the manager there, and I can call her. And I want to give those things to her, because she was kind to me at midnight, and because it is my job and I am good at it. So please: complain. Really, complain! It is not rude, and it is not a war. It is information, and without it I cannot fix anything. But say it calmly, say it early, and ask for me by name. And one more thing. Do not say sorry for complaining. Every night somebody says to me, I am so sorry to bother you, and then they apologize three more times. You are not bothering me. This is my job, and this is my hotel, and I want to know.

modals.title: Three words, three different strengths
cards: "You should speak to the manager." I am telling you what is the best thing to do. It is not an order, and it is not a maybe. It is advice.
an option -- one possible way of many
"Could you check the reservation again, please?" = a polite request. "I could try the other desk." = one possibility. Soft, open, never rude.
not sure -- maybe yes, maybe no
"The flight might be delayed." Nobody knows yet. Fifty-fifty. Use it when you are NOT certain, and nobody can say you were wrong.

items.situation: It is eleven at night in Madrid. You are tired. The receptionist looks at the screen and says your room was given to another family. Open the conversation, and do not shout.
tips: There seems to be a problem with my reservation. I booked a double room in March.
I was told that the reservation was confirmed. Could you check it again, please?

items.situation: He looks again and says there is nothing free tonight. You want the person above him. Ask, politely.
tips: I would like to speak to the manager, please.
Could I speak to the manager? I think she could help us.

items.situation: Your suitcase arrives at the hotel from the airport, and the wheel is broken. Tell the front desk what is wrong with it.
tips: My suitcase is damaged. The wheel is broken.
There seems to be a problem with my bag: it arrived damaged.

items.situation: The restaurant bill has a bottle of wine on it, and you drank water. Say it, at the table, to the waiter.
tips: Excuse me. I think we were overcharged. We did not order this wine.
There seems to be a problem with the bill. This bottle is not ours.

items.situation: You are back home and your camera is not in your suitcase. You call the hotel in Madrid. Say what is missing, and say when.
tips: I think I have a missing item: my camera. I stayed in room three one two on Saturday.
There seems to be a problem: a missing item from my room. Could you ask the cleaning staff, please?

items.situation: At the gate, the agent says your flight might be delayed for four hours. You have another flight after that one. Ask her what your options are.
tips: I have a connection in Lisbon. What could I do?
Could you put me on a different flight this evening?

items.situation: The hotel gives you a bigger room and a free breakfast because of the mistake. Accept it, and be warm about it.
tips: Thank you very much. That is very kind of you.
Thank you for the compensation, and thank you for the apology.

items.situation: Your friend tells you that you should shout, because shouting is the only thing that works. You do not think so. Say it, and say why.
tips: I disagree. In my opinion, calm is faster.
I disagree. The manager helped me because I was polite, not because I was loud.
